page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c4.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8369HB x86_64 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The CPU architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 33 MB L3 cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512F, AVX-512_VNNI, and AVX-512_BF16. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the mid-range for compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core score is 1578.98, and the multi-core score is 16657.34, both falling into the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show 921.81 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, but uncached large-block reads drop to 72.44 GB/sec, and write speeds reach 50.62 GB/sec. The Passmark memory score of 1797.16 is categorized as weak, representing a potential bottleneck for memory-intensive applications. However, the CPU handles integer and cryptographic operations efficiently, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score of 17360.29 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 26.96 GB/sec.

For database and web workloads, the instance achieves 15.83 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and a static web serving throughput of 9.51 GB/sec. Small language model inference runs at 991.37 tokens per second for prompt processing and 169.31 tokens per second for generation using a 135M parameter model. Medium model prompt processing is slower at 22.42 tokens per second, though generation maintains an average speed of 9.28 tokens per second. Given its compute-heavy 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of local storage, the ecs.c4.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as web serving, batch processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c4.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8369HB x86_64 processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network performance is anchored by a 6 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 16657.34 and a Passmark CPU score of 17360.29. While memory bandwidth is average for cached reads, uncached memory performance is relatively weak. The instance is highly capable for web serving, achieving 9.51 GB/sec throughput, and handles Redis operations at 15.83 million operations per second. It is best suited for compute-bound workloads, such as batch processing, web serving, and lightweight model inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c4.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced performance for web serving and batch processing.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c4.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated sixteen-core compute-optimized instance with a balanced memory profile for web serving and batch processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8369HB processor with 16 dedicated physical cores at 3.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Average multi-core performance with 16,657.34 stress-ng score
Suitable for web serving, Redis databases, and lightweight inference
